terms.definition: A linear data structure whose elements (nodes) are linked by pointers rather than stored in contiguous memory.
One element of a linked structure; holds a value and one or more pointers to other nodes.
A linked list where each node has only a forward pointer (`next`).
A linked list where each node has both `next` and `prev` pointers, enabling O(1) removal from any known node.
A non-data node used as a head or tail placeholder to simplify boundary conditions during insert/remove.
A cache eviction policy that discards the entry not accessed for the longest time; typically backed by a hash table + doubly-linked list.
The tortoise-and-hare algorithm: two pointers moving at different speeds detect a cycle in O(n) time and O(1) space.
